Title: Structural diversity and chemical logic underlying the assembly of monoterpene indole alkaloids oligomers

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for pathway engineering of strychnine-type MIAs and oligomer diversification; could be combined with upstream strictosidine pathway genes and malonyl-CoA supply engineering. Use with caution because evidence is discussed in a review and flux effects are context dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Incorporates a malonyl-CoA-derived unit into the strychnine biosynthetic pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of the strychnine scaffold by introducing a malonyl-CoA-derived biosynthetic element.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Referenced pathway elucidation by O'Connor and colleagues; this review cites recent elucidation rather than presenting primary experiments.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool / required structural building block for target product
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: Unknown gene(s) from the strychnine biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review explicitly states that O'Connor's recent elucidation demonstrated the strychnine scaffold relies on incorporation of a malonyl-CoA unit, confirming a long-standing biosynthetic hypothesis. No concrete gene symbol is provided in the review text.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Strychnine-pathway intermediate containing incorporated malonyl-derived carbon unit
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The review cites recent elucidation that the strychnine scaffold incorporates a malonyl-CoA-derived unit. 2. This resolves a decades-old biosynthetic assumption based on chemical logic and radiolabeling. 3. No specific gene identity is provided in the review, so only a pathway-level gene placeholder can be extracted.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant chemical defense; pharmacological and toxic bioactivity
